message.content:
Read client/src/components/HomePage/postCards/PostCards.jsx and find all places where user info is displayed (author name, avatar, VerifiedBadge). I need to know:

1. How `journal.users` data is accessed in the JSX
2. Where VerifiedBadge is rendered (exact JSX lines)
3. The data structure — what fields exist on journal.users (name, image_url, badge, etc.)

Also read client/src/components/comments/commentsCards.jsx (or similar filename) to see how comment author info is rendered, since comments.jsx delegates to CommentsCards.

Return exact file paths and relevant JSX snippets showing where user names and badges are displayed.

content.text: I'll help you find how user info is displayed in the post cards and comments components. Let me start by locating and reading these files.
Perfect! Now let me read these files to get the exact information you need.

content.text: Excellent! Now let me check the VerifiedBadge component to understand what properties it uses:

Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile my findings: ## User Info Display Analysis - iskrib_v3 ### 1. **PostCards.jsx - How `journal.users` data is accessed** **File:** `C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\HomePage\postCards\PostCards.jsx` The `journal.users` object contains user data and is accessed throughout the component. The data structure includes: - `journal.users.id` - User ID - `journal.users.name` - User's display name - `journal.users.image_url` - User's avatar/profile image URL - `journal.users.badge` - Badge type ('legend', 'og', or falsy) ### 2. **VerifiedBadge Rendering Locations in PostCards.jsx** **Line 860** - Main author badge display: ```jsx ``` Located in the user info section below the author's name. **Line 786** - Repost source author badge: ```jsx ``` Located in the embedded repost card showing the original author's badge. ### 3. **User Info Display Sections in PostCards.jsx** **Lines 855-863** - Main user info display block: ```jsx
handleClickUserProfile(e, user?.userData?.[0].id, journal.users.id)} className={`user-avatar-container ${journal.users.badge === 'legend' ? 'avatar-ring-legend' : journal.users.badge === 'og' ? 'avatar-ring-og' : ''}`}>
handleClickUserProfile(e, user?.userData?.[0].id, journal.users.id)} className="user-name-container">

{journal.users.name}

•

{formatPostDate(journal.created_at)}

``` **Lines 739-741** - Repost header user display: ```jsx handleClickUserProfile(e, user?.userData?.[0]?.id, journal.users.id)} className="repost-header-name">{journal.users.name} reposted ``` **Lines 783-786** - Repost embedded card author display: ```jsx
{repostSource.users?.name}
``` ### 4. **CommentsCards.jsx - Comment Author Info Display** **File:** `C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\comments\commentsCards.jsx` **Lines 165-181** - Comment author section with VerifiedBadge: ```jsx
clickProfile(e, user?.userData[0].id, comments?.user_id)} className={`cm-avatar-container ${comments?.users?.badge === 'legend' ? 'cm-ring-legend' : comments?.users?.badge === 'og' ? 'cm-ring-og' : ''}`} >
clickProfile(e, user?.userData[0].id, comments?.user_id)} className="cm-username" > {comments?.users?.name} · {formatPostDate(comments?.created_at)}
``` **Line 178** - VerifiedBadge rendering: ```jsx ``` ### 5. **Data Structure Summary** Both posts and comments use the same `users` object structure: - `users.id` - Unique user identifier - `users.name` - Display name - `users.image_url` - Avatar URL (fallback: `/assets/profile.jpg`) - `users.badge` - Badge type: `'legend'` | `'og'` | `null/undefined` ### 6. **VerifiedBadge Component** **File:** `C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\Badge\VerifiedBadge.jsx` Props: - `badge` (string) - Badge type: `'legend'` or `'og'` (returns null if not provided) - `size` (number, default 18) - SVG size in pixels The component renders a starburst shape with a checkmark inside. Badge styling: - **Legend:** Gold (#FFD700) with gold glow - **OG:** Purple (#9B59FF) with purple glow Both avatar containers have conditional CSS classes for badge styling: - `avatar-ring-legend` - Ring styling for legend badge - `avatar-ring-og` - Ring styling for OG badge - `cm-ring-legend` / `cm-ring-og` - Comment versions
